data.title: Top Glove Top Executives Boost Stakes With RM2.38 Million Share Purchases
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Top Glove Bhd ( (TPGVF) ) has provided an announcement.

Top Glove Corporation Bhd disclosed that its Executive Chairman, Tan Sri Dr Lim Wee Chai, and Executive Director, Mr Lim Hooi Sin, each acquired 2 million ordinary shares in the company on 26 January 2026, at a total consideration of RM1.19 million per transaction, representing 0.025% of the company’s shares apiece. The off-closed-period insider purchases, involving both indirect and direct interests, signal continued confidence by top management in the group’s prospects and may be interpreted by investors as a supportive move for the stock’s valuation and market perception.

More about Top Glove Bhd

Top Glove Corporation Bhd is a Malaysian-listed manufacturer best known as one of the world’s largest producers of rubber gloves, supplying medical, industrial and household glove products to global markets. The company operates in the healthcare and protective equipment industry, with a strong export-oriented focus and a significant presence on Bursa Malaysia under the stock name TOPGLOV.

For an in-depth examination of TPGVF stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Tianjin Development Sells Entire Stake in Benefo Financial Leasing for RMB265 Million
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Tianjin Development Holdings ( (HK:0882) ) is now available.

Tianjin Development Holdings has agreed to dispose of its entire equity interest in Benefo Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. through its 82.74%-owned subsidiary Tianjin Tai Kang, which will sell 100% of Benefo Financial Leasing to Tianjin Capital for RMB264.93 million (approximately HK$294.37 million), based on an asset-based valuation as at 30 September 2025 and in compliance with PRC state-owned asset transfer rules. Following completion and the required regulatory and state-owned asset approvals, Benefo Financial Leasing will cease to be a subsidiary of the group, with Tianjin Capital assuming all profits and losses from 30 September 2025 to completion, marking an adjustment to Tianjin Development’s portfolio exposure to financial leasing while meeting Hong Kong Listing Rules disclosure requirements for a discloseable transaction.

More about Tianjin Development Holdings

Tianjin Development Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong–incorporated investment holding company with diversified operations through subsidiaries, including financial services activities conducted via entities such as Benefo Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. The group leverages its platform and state-owned enterprise affiliations to participate in regulated sectors in mainland China, including financial leasing and related asset-based financing services.

Average Trading Volume: 374,267

Current Market Cap: HK$2.8B

For a thorough assessment of 0882 stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

The latest announcement is out from Hongkong Land Holdings ( (GB:HKLD) ).

Hongkong Land Holdings Limited has repurchased 175,000 of its ordinary shares on 26 January 2026 at prices ranging between US$8.21 and US$8.35 per share, with a weighted average price of US$8.2844, and the repurchased shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, the company’s issued share capital stands at 2,156,029,126 ordinary shares with one vote per share and no treasury shares held, a structure that may support earnings per share and capital management objectives while providing clarity for shareholders’ disclosure and voting-right calculations.

data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The latest announcement is out from Low Keng Huat Singapore Ltd ( (SG:F1E) ).

Low Keng Huat (Singapore) Limited has placed its indirect wholly owned Malaysian subsidiary, Vista Mutiara Sdn. Bhd., into members’ voluntary liquidation and appointed liquidators to wind up the entity. The company stated that the liquidation is not expected to have any material impact on its earnings per share or net tangible assets for the financial year ending 31 January 2026, and confirmed that none of its directors or substantial or controlling shareholders has any interest in the process, suggesting the move is an internal housekeeping exercise with limited implications for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (SG:F1E) stock is a Hold with a S$0.75 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Low Keng Huat Singapore Ltd stock, see the SG:F1E Stock Forecast page.

Low Keng Huat (Singapore) Limited is a Singapore-incorporated group with operations structured through various subsidiaries, including property-related entities in regional markets such as Malaysia. The company manages its portfolio through direct and indirect wholly owned units, periodically restructuring its holdings to streamline operations and optimise capital use across the group.

data.title: China Overseas Property Sees 2025 Revenue Growth but Profit Hit by Restructuring and Margin Pressure
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from China Overseas Property Holdings ( (HK:2669) ).

China Overseas Property Holdings has flagged that its unaudited revenue for the year ended 31 December 2025 rose by about 5–7% versus 2024, broadly in line with its development expectations despite difficult internal and external economic conditions. Management attributed the top-line growth to flexible responses to market changes and adjustments in business strategy, while noting that newly expanded city services continued to deliver both revenue and profit growth.

However, the company warned that transformation and restructuring efforts required substantial resource inputs and extended payback periods, weighing on profitability. Gross profit and profit attributable to ordinary equity holders are expected to fall by about 3–4% and 9–10% respectively, with higher amortisation of R&D spending on intelligent engineering projects pushing its value-added services to non-residents segment into loss for the first time, and aggressive price competition eroding margins in other engineering businesses. Traditional property management services also came under pressure from fee reductions, contract withdrawals and slower contract conversion amid the broader real estate downturn, underscoring the margin risk facing property service providers even as they seek growth in new business lines such as city services. The company’s final audited 2025 results are due by end-March 2026, and investors have been cautioned about trading its shares in the interim.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2669) stock is a Hold with a HK$5.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Overseas Property Holdings stock, see the HK:2669 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Overseas Property Holdings

China Overseas Property Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed property management and related services group, with operations centered on traditional residential property management, value-added services to non-residents such as intelligent engineering projects, and an expanding portfolio of municipal and city services. The company operates in the broader mainland China property services sector, where it competes on service quality and price amid a prolonged downturn in the real estate market and intensifying price competition across engineering and management contracts.

Average Trading Volume: 6,785,933

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$15.99B

For a thorough assessment of 2669 stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: NCAB Group Schedules Webcast for 2025 Annual Accounts and Q4 Report
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NCAB Group AB ( (SE:NCAB) ) has provided an update.

NCAB Group will publish its annual accounts and fourth-quarter report for 2025 on Friday, 13 February 2026 at 07:30 CET, and will host a webcasted telephone conference at 10:00 CET the same day, led by CEO Peter Kruk and CFO Timothy Benjamin, followed by a Q&A session. The presentation, held in English and accessible online or by phone, will be accompanied by slide material released ahead of the call and a recorded version made available afterward on the company’s website, underscoring NCAB’s efforts to maintain transparency and active dialogue with investors and other stakeholders around its financial performance and strategic development.

The most recent analyst rating on (SE:NCAB) stock is a Hold with a SEK50.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on NCAB Group AB stock, see the SE:NCAB Stock Forecast page.

More about NCAB Group AB

NCAB Group AB is a Nasdaq Stockholm–listed, worldwide leading supplier of printed circuit boards (PCBs) for demanding industrial customers. Founded in 1993, the company focuses on delivering high-quality, sustainably produced PCBs on time and at the lowest total cost, and has built an entrepreneurial, cost-efficient culture that has supported strong growth and profitability. NCAB operates with a local presence in 19 countries across Europe, Asia and North America, and reported revenues of SEK 3,614 million in 2024, pursuing both organic growth and acquisitions as part of its strategy.

Average Trading Volume: 195,492

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: SEK8.66B

data.title: Babylon Pump & Power Clears Lapsed and Expired Performance Rights from Capital Structure
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Babylon Pump & Power Ltd ( (AU:BPP) ) just unveiled an update.

Babylon Pump & Power Ltd has notified the market of the cessation of certain performance rights on issue under its capital structure, as disclosed in an Appendix 3H filing. A total of 13,889 Class I performance rights lapsed because their conditions were not met or became incapable of being satisfied, while 125,000 Class J performance rights expired unexercised, resulting in a modest reduction in the company’s pool of potential equity and clarifying its issued capital position for shareholders and the market.
